memory.summary: That scholarship money could really help, but I'm tempted to just have fun now since nobody is waiting on me. It feels like a waste of time though, even if the deadline isn't right now.
My energy is dipping and I'm feeling that familiar urge to skip the scholarship work for fun, even though I know it's a waste of time.

desires: Stop feeling like a burden to my team and get back into the loop before they think I've checked out.
Finish this scholarship essay so I can stop letting financial insecurity hang over our group projects.

goals.description: Finish the one-paragraph essay snippet immediately to stop feeling like a traitor to my team and prove I'm not ghosting them.

Even though I want to game, the guilt spiral is too heavy right now. goals.description: Start the scholarship application by drafting the intro paragraph so I can claim 'I started it' as an excuse to game tonight without looking irresponsible.
goals.rationale: My impulsive nature (planning horizon: short, procrastination bias: 7) and risk tolerance (7) are screaming for fun_now. However, I know that if I don't do *something* productive first, the guilt will ruin my gaming session anyway. I'll defer this until after the essay snippet is done, using the completed work as a moral shield to play.
